Update architecture diagram via draw.io desktop editor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix latency monitor for serverless: remove cache(), DESCRIBE DETAIL, saveAsTable

Serverless compute doesn't support PERSIST TABLE (used by cache(), DESCRIBE DETAIL,
and saveAsTable to SDP-managed schemas). Fixed by:
- Replacing cache() with collect() once
- Estimating sizes from row counts (avg bytes/row per layer)
- Removing Delta table logging (results shown in notebook output only)

kind-pasteur-2026-03-06-S25c: augmented tournament and consec analysis

- M[a,b] = signed count of Ham paths in T'=T+{a->b} using added edge
- Consec palindrome holds at n=7 (all circulants) but not all n=5
- Alternating sum always vanishes regardless of palindrome property
- The algebraic proof of NONHAM=0 at general n remains open

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

opus-2026-03-06-S26: even r-powers confirmed — M(r) = M(-r) verified

THM-030 Corollary 2 confirmed computationally:
- M[a,b](r) is an EVEN function of r (only r^0, r^2 terms at n=3)
- M(r) = M(-r) verified for both 3-cycle and transitive at n=3
- Corrected consecutive formula holds at ALL values of r, not just r=1/2
- The polynomial structure: 3-cycle M[a,a] = 1/2 + 2r^2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: スマホ音声認識の無限ループを修正 (#27)

【原因】
iOS Safari / Android Chrome は continuous=true を無視し、
各発話後に recognition が自動停止する。これにより:
1. 発話終了 → onend → recognition.start()
2. question 変更で useEffect cleanup → recognition.stop() → onend 再発火
3. onend → recognition.start() → ∞ループ
の競合が発生していた。

【対応】
- shouldRestartRecognitionRef: 再開意図フラグを導入
  - onend は true の場合のみ再開（意図的 stop では false になる）
- isRecognitionRunningRef: 現在の起動状態を追跡
  - question 変更時は既に起動中なら start() を呼ばない
- useEffect cleanup での stop() を廃止
  - question 変更時は認識を継続させ onresult ハンドラのみ更新
  - 意図的停止（voiceInput OFF / ゲーム終了）は else 分岐でフラグを先に
    false にしてから stop() → onend が自動再開しない
- アンマウント専用 useEffect([]) を追加してコンポーネント破棄時に確実停止
- モバイル対応: onend 再開に 150ms の待機を追加（即時再開が失敗するため）

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Vite dev proxy doesn't apply in production. Without this,

commit.message:
Fix slow reorder: timer now based on last click, not last API response

Timer was resetting in the finally block (after each API response),
so 5 rapid votes could delay reorder by seconds as each response
pushed the timer back. Now the 800ms timer is set on each click
(synchronous), and just waits for in-flight calls to finish before
fetching. Result: reorder happens ~800ms after your last click +
whatever time the last API call needs to finish.

commit.message:
[frontend] Improve chart emphasis visibility and color palette (#272)

* feat: add dark outline to emphasized chart curves and improve color palette

Replace the 3rd row of colors (which were just lighter/darker variants of
rows 1-2) with genuinely distinct hues for better visual distinction between
runs. Add a subtle dark outline to emphasized curves in the draw hook so
hovered lines are immediately identifiable, especially in dense charts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add PR screenshots for chart emphasis and palette changes

* fix: stabilize flaky E2E tests for graph rendering and shareable URLs

- graph-rendering: use toPass() auto-retry for empty state/ReactFlow
  visibility check instead of a single snapshot that may miss loading
- url-params-selection: use a fresh browser context (isolated IndexedDB)
  for the shareable URL test instead of clearing cache in the same
  context, which races with the original page's debounced cache writes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: make dimmed chart curves visible on dark backgrounds

The emphasis dimming used alpha 0.25 for non-hovered curves, which
made them nearly invisible on dark backgrounds. Use theme-aware
alpha values (0.5 for dark, 0.3 for light) so dimmed curves remain
clearly colored while still being visually secondary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: increase emphasis dimming visibility and outline strength

Bump dark-mode dim alpha from 0.5 to 0.55 and outline opacity from
0.55 to 0.7 with wider stroke (3px vs 2.5px per side). Dimmed curves
now clearly show their color on dark backgrounds instead of appearing
near-black.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: keep dimmed curves visible by raising minimum line width

The emphasis dimming reduced line width to 0.7px, making curves
invisible on dark backgrounds regardless of alpha. Raise minimum
dimmed width to 1.2px (from 0.7px) in both local and cross-chart
emphasis paths. Also set dim alpha to 0.5 for dark / 0.3 for light.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: fix cross-chart emphasis causing curves to disappear

Root causes:
1. Cross-chart highlight used React state (async) to update refs, but
   the imperative redraw happened before state propagated — stroke
   functions read stale ref values during redraw
2. uPlot's built-in focus.alpha (0.3) stacked with custom stroke
   dimming (0.5), giving ~15% effective alpha — nearly invisible
3. Dark outline on highlighted curve (6px wide, 70% black) covered
   dimmed curves following the same path
4. Static band fills didn't dim during emphasis, creating dark masses

Fixes:
- Store cross-chart highlight run ID directly on uPlot instances
  for synchronous access during imperative redraws
- Use chart.redraw() (full) instead of redraw(false) so stroke
  functions are always re-evaluated
- Set focus.alpha to 1 to disable uPlot's built-in dimming
- Only draw dark outline on locally-hovered chart, not cross-chart
- Make envelope band fills dynamic (dim for non-highlighted runs)
- Tune dim alpha to 0.25 (dark) / 0.2 (light) for clear emphasis

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: unify dark outline across all emphasis types (local, cross-chart, table)

The dark outline now draws consistently for local hover, cross-chart
hover, and table row hover. Previously it was only shown for local
hover to avoid a dark mass issue — now fixed by filtering out envelope
boundary and raw companion series from the outline pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: scale emphasis outline width proportionally to user line width

The dark outline behind emphasized curves now uses
max(2, lineWidth * 1.5) instead of a fixed 3px, so it scales
with the user's line width setting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: make emphasis line widths proportional to user setting

Highlighted: max(2, lw × 1.4), dimmed: max(0.8, lw × 0.6).
Previously 2× / 0.8× which created too large a jump, especially
at higher line width settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: tighten emphasis width ratio to ~1.47×

Highlighted: lw × 1.25, dimmed: lw × 0.85. Gives a subtle ~1.47×
ratio between highlighted and dimmed at all line width settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: lower emphasis width minimums so thin lines keep ~1.47× ratio

Minimums: highlighted 1px, dimmed 0.4px (was 1.8/0.8). Proportional
scaling now dominates at all practical line width settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: assign sequential palette colors to selected runs only

Instead of hashing all run IDs to pick colors (which could give
adjacent similar colors to a small selection), colors are now assigned
sequentially from the palette to selected runs only. This ensures
2-3 selected runs always get maximally distinct colors (e.g. cyan,
green, orange). Unselected runs show a neutral gray dot in the table.

When a run is deselected, its color slot is freed and reused by the
next newly selected run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent init effect re-run from overriding URL param selection

Add initializedRef flag to prevent the initialization effect from
re-running and applying default 5-run selection when selectedRunsRef
hasn't caught up with the state set by the URL change effect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Kelly's Maximum Contrast palette and palette selector UI

Add a 24-color categorical palette based on Kelly's Maximum Contrast
colors, optimized for perceptual distinctiveness. Users can switch
between "Categorical" (Kelly) and "Vivid" (existing) palettes via a
toggle in the color picker popover. Preference is stored in localStorage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use functional state update in run sync effect to prevent race

The "keep run objects in sync" effect was reading selectedRunsRef.current
(stale cached data) and overriding URL-param-driven selection set in the
same render cycle. Using a functional update to setSelectedRunsWithColors
ensures it reads the latest state, including updates from the URL params
effect that ran earlier in the same batch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: move palette selector to visibility options as global control

The palette toggle (Categorical/Vivid) was inside the per-run ColorPicker
popover which caused two issues: (1) the popover closed on switch due to
re-render, and (2) it only affected single run colors. Now the palette
selector lives in the Visibility Options dropdown and switching palettes
reassigns sequential colors to ALL selected runs from the new palette.

Adds E2E test verifying palette switch changes colors, dropdown stays
open, and switching back restores original colors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add dark-mode variant of Kelly categorical palette

The original Kelly palette has colors (#803E75, #00538A, #53377A,
#7F180D, #2C4A6E) that are too dark on black backgrounds. Added
COLORS_KELLY_DARK with the same hue order but brightened so every
color pops on dark surfaces. The Categorical palette is now theme-aware:
dark mode uses COLORS_KELLY_DARK, light mode uses COLORS_KELLY.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: reassign run colors when theme changes palette

The color dots and chart curves kept stale hex values when switching
between light/dark mode. Now when chartColors changes (theme or palette
type), all selected runs are reassigned sequential colors from the new
palette automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove PR screenshot images from repo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add stale-interaction detection instead of wall-clock timeout

Instead of a hard 5-minute timeout that would kill legitimate
long-running research, track the API's `updated` timestamp. If it
hasn't changed in 3 minutes, the interaction is likely stuck — cancel
it and surface a clear error. This lets 10+ minute research run
uninterrupted while still catching hung/stale interactions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
S3-based dashboard deploys: build frontend → upload tar.gz → restart ECS

The Docker image no longer bakes in the frontend. On startup, the
entrypoint downloads frontend.tar.gz from S3 and extracts it. This
makes frontend-only deploys ~30s instead of ~6min.

- Dockerfile: remove frontend build stage, add AWS CLI for S3 downloads
- entrypoint.sh: download + extract tar.gz from DASHBOARD_ASSETS_S3
- update_cli: default path builds Next.js locally, tars, uploads to S3,
  restarts ECS. --docker flag for full image rebuild (backend changes)
- CDK stack: pass DASHBOARD_ASSETS_S3 env var to container

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

security: add auth to merchant endpoints, CORS for checkout.sardis.sh

- Add require_principal auth to merchant management router (was open)
- Add require_principal auth to merchant checkout session creation

visually outside the toggle block with only margin-left indentation.

for blocks inside the container (visual nesting replaces indent).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: corregir preview y descarga de PDFs en facturas y comprobantes

El problema raíz era que Cloudinary perdía la extensión .pdf del archivo
porque se eliminaba del public_id al subir. Esto causaba una cadena de
fallos: backend devolvía MIME type incorrecto, frontend no detectaba que
era PDF y lo renderizaba como imagen, y la descarga no tenía extensión.

Cambios backend:
- file_storage.py: preservar extensión en public_id de Cloudinary
- expenses.py, payments.py, contributions.py: detectar MIME por magic
  bytes (%PDF-), extensión, o header de Cloudinary. Agregar .pdf al
  filename si falta. Contributions ahora soporta proxy de Cloudinary.

Cambios frontend:
- ExpenseDetail, MyPayments, PendingApprovals, ContributionDetail:
  usar response.data directo (ya es Blob), detectar tipo del blob
  para asegurar extensión correcta en fileName y preview.

Funciona tanto para archivos nuevos (con extensión) como existentes
(sin extensión, detectados por magic bytes).

  research starts (triggered by deepResearch payload on generating)

  log stays clean: "Researching... (1m 30s elapsed)"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

opus-2026-03-06-S26: palindromic defect landscape at n=5

Key findings:
- All 8 scalar M tilings at defect=0 have H=15 exclusively
- Max BFS distance to nearest scalar is 3 (every tiling close)
- Greedy descent succeeds 85.9% (9 stuck at local minima at H=1,3)
- Palindromic defect anti-correlated with H: H=15→0, H=5/11→24
- Palindromic N ⟺ scalar M confirmed (no exceptions)
